title: The Whispering Lamp in Whitby Study
openingIncident: At 8:55 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned antiquarian and museum curator, was found dead in a cosy study with dark wood paneling, a lamp, a book, a mug and an armchair. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Hargreaves
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, Percival Thorne helped me move the chair clear of the doorway. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Miranda Llewelyn and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Percival Thorne and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

suspects.name: Miranda Llewelyn
suspects.statement: I left at 8:40 pm. I never touched the lamp and did not go near the book.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:40 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Percival Thorne
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, I helped Gareth Hargreaves move the chair clear of the doorway. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:55 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Hargreaves and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

clues.title: The Dented Lamp
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the lamp. Eleanor Whitcombe has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Movement of Chair
clues.detail: A fresh floor scrape made by the chair is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Hargreaves and Percival Thorne both state that they moved the chair clear of the doorway at 8:45 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Lamp
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught inside a fresh split beneath the base of the lamp. Miranda Llewelyn's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Book
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged between the cover and first page of the book on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Miranda Llewelyn has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Mug
clues.detail: A crack runs across the mug. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Miranda Llewelyn killed Eleanor Whitcombe because she had discovered his embezzlement of museum funds.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitcombe was killed by a heavy blow from the lamp.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:45 pm and before discovery at 8:55 pm
solution.opportunity: Miranda Llewelyn claimed to have left at 8:40 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Gareth Hargreaves and Percival Thorne left together at 8:45 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the lamp and the black and gold fragment recovered from the book place Miranda Llewelyn inside afterwards. Miranda Llewelyn therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitcombe and reach the lamp during the murder window.
